message:
🐛 Fix scroll snap picking wrong card on same grid row (#219)

The snap-to-card logic used a tolerance of 8px to detect which card
was at the viewport top. However, scroll restoration adds 12px of
breathing room above the card, placing the card top 12px below
scrollTop. This exceeded the 8px tolerance, causing the snap to
select the card above (e.g., "Cluster Groups" instead of
"Deployment Missions").

Additionally, in a 12-column grid layout, multiple cards share the
same row. The old loop picked the LAST card within tolerance (rightmost
in DOM order), but the correct behavior is to pick the FIRST card on
the topmost visible row.

Changes:
- Increase snap tolerance from 8px to 20px (covers 12px breathing room
  plus buffer for sub-pixel rounding)
- Detect row boundaries (>2px position difference) and keep only the
  first card on each row, ensuring left-most card is saved

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
